channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: New Orleans Pelicans tap Joe Dumars to lead basketball operations
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The New Orleans Pelicans have hired Joe Dumars — a former NBA champion as both a player and an executive with the Detroit Pistons — as their executive vice president of basketball operations.
item.dc:creator: BRETT MARTEL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Golden State Warriors warn fans of counterfeit playoff tickets
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Warriors narrowly clinched a spot in the 2025 NBA Playoffs after a home win Tuesday night against the Memphis Grizzlies. Scammers, the team warned in a new advisory, are also looking to win big by selling fans counterfeit tickets.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: Scottie Scheffler explains awkward Masters green jacket exchange with Rory McIlroy
item.description: Scottie Scheffler wanted Rory McIlroy to have his moments on Sunday after the PGA Tour star completed the career grand slam when he won the Masters in a one-hole playoff.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fencer reveals difficulties after taking stand on trans inclusion in women's sports
item.description: Fencer Stephanie Turner expanded on some of the difficulties she has experienced since she protested against trans inclusion in women's sports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Baseball celebrates Jackie Robinson's legacy on 78th anniversary of breaking color barrier
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Jackie Robinson was the first to break baseball's color barrier with the Brooklyn Dodgers 78 years ago Tuesday. His legacy continues to inspire people inside the major leagues — and outside, too.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: NFL teams are no longer shying away from older prospects in the draft
item.description: Bo Nix has already turned 25. Michael Penix Jr. will get there in May. Both quarterbacks were first-round picks last year. They were among 56 players age 24 or older drafted in 2024. That number was up from 54 in 2023, 44 in 2022, 16 in 2021 and 19 in 2020.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA star Kevin Durant takes shot at Stephen A Smith amid questions over leadership
item.description: Phoenix Suns star Kevin Durant threw shade at ESPN's Stephen A. Smith on Tuesday as the two traded barbs over leadership amid the team's poor season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Young Bulls, Lonzo Ball and future of his highly-touted twin sons with Carlos Boozer
item.description: Carlos Boozer recently spoke with Jarrett Payton to discuss the Chicago Bulls' young team, Lonzo Ball's injury, and his sons' basketball success, with both Cameron and Cayden Boozer being highly-touted high school recruits and planning to attend Duke University.
item.dc:creator: Jarrett Payton, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz close out season with 116-105 loss at Minnesota
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (ABC4 Sports) - The NBA season has mercifully come to an end for the Utah Jazz. The Jazz ended the worst season in franchise history with a 116-105 loss to the Minnesota Timberwolves, who were led by Anthony Edwards with 43 points.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: NBA's West spots set, with the Nuggets, Clippers and Timberwolves in and the Warriors play-in bound
item.description: The Los Angeles Clippers worked overtime to clinch a playoff spot — and send the Golden State Warriors to the NBA's play-in tournament.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Western Michigan wins its 1st Frozen Four hockey title, beating Boston University 6-2
item.description: Owen Michaels didn’t hesitate after watching his shot slide into the open net with more than two minutes left to essentially seal Western Michigan’s first Frozen Four title in its first championship game.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW, Associated Press, Fox 5
